floor_history.headline: recon: out[4]->out[3] predicted byte-identical (BLKmode 8-align + MIPS_STACK_ALIGN both round 0x1C->0x20); PsyQ VECTOR (vx,vy,vz,pad) is the semantically-real alternative

frontier.hypothesis: s32 out[3]; alone is byte-identical to the current out[4] form — the dead 4th element contributes nothing to frame layout
frontier.mechanism: cc1 frame layout: assign_stack_local gives BLKmode arrays 8-byte alignment (function.c ~678, BIGGEST_ALIGNMENT=64) so out sits at sp+0x20 regardless; mips.c compute_frame_size MIPS_STACK_ALIGN rounds the 0x1C locals span back to 0x20, keeping frame 0x38 and $ra at sp+0x30
frontier.next_probe: Edit src/ings.c:783 `s32 out[4];` -> `s32 out[3];`, run `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox math_Distance3D --disable all` expecting 0, then full verify-oracle; if it holds, apply the same one-line fix to math_Distance3D_16 (src/ings.c:793)

frontier.hypothesis: If out[3] diverges (contradicting the arithmetic), a VECTOR-style struct local `struct { s32 vx, vy, vz, pad; } out;` reaches the 16-byte slot with a semantically-real PsyQ SDK type instead of a dead array element
frontier.mechanism: Same frame-layout pass; a 16-byte RECORD_TYPE local gets the identical BLKmode 8-aligned slot as s32[4], but the 4th word is the SDK's documented pad member — human-natural GTE code, not frame coercion
frontier.next_probe: Only if probe 1 fails: swap the local to the 4-word struct (members accessed as out.vx+out.vy+out.vz), sandbox expecting 0, and record which of the two spellings the judge accepts as clean

frontier.hypothesis: If both respellings shift layout, the divergence is in var_size rounding, not the array — read the actual computed frame constants from an RTL dump before touching anything else
frontier.mechanism: cc1 -da .greg dump prints the frame layout decisions (compute_frame_size result, slot offsets); diff out[3] vs out[4] dumps to see the first differing constant
frontier.next_probe: cpp the TU, run tools/gcc-2.7.2/build/cc1 <build flags> -da on both variants, diff the .greg frame dispositions for math_Distance3D

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): out[4] has a dead 4th element (out[3] is never written by the callee or read by the caller) that inflates the array to 16 bytes to coerce $ra to sp+0x30 and the frame to 0x38 — frame-coercion by dead array element. Worker must find a pure-C form that reaches the correct frame layout without the dead element, or confirm via GCC run whether out[3] produces the same layout.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
